summary: The current guidelines strongly recommend preoperative tracheobronchoscopy in neonates with esophageal atresia to accurately assess airway anatomy, identify potential complications such as tracheomalacia or abnormal vascular relationships, and guide safe surgical intervention by determining precise anatomic landmarks including fistula location and aortic arch position. Performing this procedure is essential prior to surgery due to its critical role in ensuring both procedural accuracy and patient safety during corrective procedures.
